commit.message:
Create shared SDK types (#298)

* Add wasm-meta-registry-types and rich API

Extract shared wire types into a new crate `wasm-meta-registry-types` and update the workspace/Cargo manifests. The client crate now depends on and re-exports the types crate; several new rich client methods were added (fetch_package_detail, fetch_package_versions, fetch_package_version, search by import/export) along with helper fetch_* functions. Server routes and handlers were added/nested to support package detail, versions, specific-version (reordered path), and interface-based search; routing was reorganized to avoid wildcard conflicts. The package manager and storage layers were updated to use the new types (including a ManifestRow helper) and new manager methods to return detailed package/version information. README and tests updated to reflect the move.

* Add package kind (component/interface) support

Introduce PackageKind enum and an optional kind field for KnownPackage and PackageDetail to track whether a package is a runnable component or a WIT interface. Persist kind in the database (added kind column and migration), update OCI upsert to accept/preserve kind (COALESCE), and wire the value through storage, manager, indexer and client code. Added parse_kind helper to map DB values to PackageKind and updated tests/examples to include kind = None for backward compatibility.

* more

* Fix Tracey coverage annotations for rich registry APIs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: force-remove stale MSI registry + clear package cache in pre-clean

When msiexec /x fails (e.g. pip's files already deleted), the product
stays 'Present' in the WiX bundle's view. The bundle then enters modify
mode and plans lib_AllUsers as execute:None/uncache:Yes — leaving Python
with no stdlib after a fresh install.

Fixes:
- Add MSIFASTINSTALL=7 to msiexec /x so missing-file uninstalls succeed
- On msiexec failure, force-delete the uninstall key + MSI UserData +
  Installer\Products tracking keys (via squished GUID conversion)
- Clear Python package cache dirs (cached:Complete lib.msi caused wrong
  bundle planning) and .unverified folder

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

EXP-1281-1290: therapy detection pivot — basal over-delivery, ISF deconfounding

10 therapy detection experiments across 11 patients (~180 days each):
- EXP-1281: Time-block basal adequacy (mean score 45.1/100)
- EXP-1282: Per-meal CR scoring (mean 49.1/100)
- EXP-1283: ISF effective estimation (2.66x profile — AID confound)
- EXP-1284: Loop compensation (9/11 suspension-dominant, 89.9% aggressive)
- EXP-1285: Multi-day tracking (4 improving, 4 degrading, 3 stable)
- EXP-1286: Prediction error signal (5.8% large errors, 13 clusters avg)
- EXP-1287: Settings recommendations (33 decrease_basal, 16 decrease_cr)
- EXP-1288: DIA adequacy (mean suggested 5.3h)
- EXP-1289: Temporal ISF profiling (0/11 dawn — needs actual ISF)
- EXP-1290: Cross-patient benchmarking (composite 27-74 range)

Key finding: 10/11 patients have basal too high, loop suspends 48-96%.
ISF effective/profile ratio of 2.66x reveals AID amplification confound.

Allow CORS requests from www.metafaux.com (#42)

The frontend at `www.metafaux.com/klaxon` is blocked from loading API
data because `https://www.metafaux.com` is not in the CORS allowed
origins list.

## Changes

- **`.env.example`** — Added
`CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://www.metafaux.com`; production deployments
need this env var set to enable cross-origin access
- **`phpunit.xml`** — Injected `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` for the test
environment
- **`tests/Feature/CorsTest.php`** — Added CORS tests covering:
- Preflight (`OPTIONS`) and actual `GET` requests from the allowed
origin receive the correct `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header
- Disallowed origins are not echoed back (browser will block the
request)

## How it works

`config/cors.php` already reads from `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` via `env()`:

```php
'allowed_origins' => array_values(array_filter(array_map('trim', explode(',', env('CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS', ''))))),
```

No config changes needed — only the environment variable was missing.

commit.message:
Rebase of useful functionality for mesh embedding (#287)

* Initial plan

* Add partial_cut mode to cut_mesh and layered_mesh functionality

- Added partial_cut keyword to cut_mesh (:positive/:negative/:none)
  for keeping only one side of the cut plane
- Added depth_grid_to_surface to convert depth grids to PolygonalSurface
- Added layered_mesh to build layered reservoir models from surfaces
- Added _point_below_surface helper for classifying cell layers
- Updated module exports and imports

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix partial_cut boundary normals and add comprehensive tests

- Reverse cut face node ordering when partial_cut=:positive to ensure
  correct outward boundary normal orientation
- Handle edge case where plane is outside mesh with partial_cut
- Add tests for partial_cut, depth_grid_to_surface, and layered_mesh

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: simplify _depth_valid, remove empty branches, add comment

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix layered_mesh hanging: use bounding polygons and fix cut face construction

Two fixes for the layered_mesh hanging issue:

1. Use each polygon as a bounding_polygon when cutting by a PolygonalSurface.
   Previously each polygon's cutting plane extended to infinity, causing
   O(n_polygons) redundant cuts per cell and exponential mesh growth.
   Now each polygon only cuts cells within its spatial footprint.

2. Fix cut face construction for cells adjacent to already-cut neighbors.
   When a non-split face has nodes on the cutting plane (from a previous
   cut), those nodes must still be collected into the cut face polygon.
   Without this fix, the cut face was incomplete, causing volume loss.

Also simplified layered_mesh to delegate to cut_mesh(mesh, surface)
instead of manually iterating over individual polygons.

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: tighten time limit, document return type

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add merge_coplanar_faces post-processing to cut cell meshes

- New merge_faces.jl: merge_coplanar_faces function that merges interior
  and boundary faces sharing ≥2 nodes, same cell pair/cell, and coplanar
  normals. Re-orders merged polygon nodes for correct orientation.
- New merge_faces keyword (default true) on cut_mesh for both PlaneCut
  and PolygonalSurface methods, passed through to build_cut_mesh.
- Tests for merge on/off, orientation, geometry preservation, extra_out.

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: rename union-find, optimize point lookup, add boundary merge test

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add embed_mesh function for embedding one mesh inside another

Algorithm:
1. Collect boundary face polygons/normals from mesh B
2. Cut mesh A with each B boundary face (keeping both sides)
3. Remove cells of cut-A whose centroids are inside B
4. Glue trimmed A with B using glue_mesh
5. Track cell origins (mesh_a/mesh_b) and original indices

Includes _remove_cells helper and _point_inside_mesh ray-casting test.

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for embed_mesh: aligned, misaligned, protruding, outside, no extra_out

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: fix step numbering and add comment for epsilon scaling

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add merge_split_cells, merge_small_cells, and fix merge_coplanar_faces

- merge_split_cells: merges cells from same original cell in same layer
- merge_small_cells: merges cells below volume threshold with best neighbor
- merge_coplanar_faces: add same-plane check, convexity check, fix moved
  done-marking after convexity check
- layered_mesh gains merge_cells keyword (default true)
- _dedup_nodes helper removes consecutive duplicate nodes in face polygons

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for merge_split_cells, merge_small_cells, and convexity check

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: use face diagonal for coplanarity tolerance scaling

Co-authored-by: moyner <454871+moyner@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* Fix embed_mesh: fix symbol key bugs, remove layered_mesh/depth_grid_to_surface

Switch agentic workflows from pull_request_target to pull_request with fork guard (#38035)

## Security: Replace `pull_request_target` with `pull_request` in
agentic workflows

Switches all 7 agentic review workflows (archie, dash, dexter,
mgmt-review, scribe, sentinel, tester) from `pull_request_target` to
`pull_request` to reduce their privilege surface.

### Why this matters

`pull_request_target` runs in the **base branch context** with full
access to repository secrets and a write-enabled `GITHUB_TOKEN` — even
for PRs from forks. This is an unnecessary privilege escalation for
workflows that only perform read-only AI-powered code reviews.

Using `pull_request` instead follows the [principle of least
privilege](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-for-github-actions/security-guides/security-hardening-for-github-actions#understanding-the-risk-of-script-injections):
no secrets are exposed for fork PRs and the token is read-only.

### What changed

1. **Trigger**: `pull_request_target` → `pull_request` across all 7
workflow `.md` and `.lock.yml` files.
2. **Fork guard**: Added `github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork ==
false` to job conditions so workflows skip gracefully on forked PRs
(where secrets are unavailable) instead of failing.
3. **Removed workaround**: Deleted the `pull_request_target` checkout
instructions from `mgmt-review.md` that were only needed because the old
trigger checked out the base branch instead of the PR branch.

### References

- [GitHub Security Lab — Preventing pwn
requests](https://securitylab.github.com/resources/github-actions-preventing-pwn-requests/)
- [GitHub Docs — Security hardening for GitHub
Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-for-github-actions/security-guides/security-hardening-for-github-actions)
- [GitHub Blog — `pull_request_target` and branch protection
changes](https://github.blog/changelog/2025-11-07-actions-pull_request_target-and-environment-branch-protections-changes/)

### Packages impacted

None — workflow/CI changes only.

Log actual error detail on Microsoft cloud-save and proxy 502s

Graph API error to the log — making failures invisible in Fly logs.

fix(maglev): update register-file ctx slot on PushContext/PopContext

PushContext and PopContext stubs update the TLS context but did not
update the register-file ctx cache at [R14 + ctx_regfile_offset].
This caused StoreCurrentContextSlot/LoadCurrentContextSlot to read
a stale (null) pointer, deopting every function that creates a new
context (e.g. make_counter in closure_counter benchmark).

Fix: after each PushContext/PopContext stub call, call a new helper
jit_runtime_get_current_ctx_raw_ptr() and store the result at the
register-file ctx offset.

Also fix smi_guarded propagation to skip ctrl_consumed nodes, which
prevented boolean LoadKeyedGeneric values from leaking through Phi
nodes and crashing sieve_primes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Split appverifier CTest into separate parallel job (#384)

* Split AppVerifier CTest into separate parallel job

When with_app_verifier is true, build_and_run_tests.yml now generates
two parallel jobs instead of running AppVerifier CTest sequentially:

1. Main job: Build + VSTest + CTest (no AppVerifier)
2. AppVerifier job: Build + CTest with AppVerifier only

This reduces pipeline wall time by running both concurrently. For zrpc,
the critical path drops from ~41 minutes to ~25 minutes.

Changes:
- New build_setup_steps.yml: Reusable step template with the common
  build setup (checkout, submodules, nuget, toolchain, cmake, msbuild).
- New build_teardown_steps.yml: Reusable step template with teardown
  (stop trace collection, publish artifacts on failure, clean up).
- build_and_run_tests.yml: Conditionally emits a second AppVerifier job
  using the same conditions that already existed for inline AV steps.
  No new parameters needed in build_all_flavors.yml.
- build_all_flavors.yml: Unchanged (just passes with_app_verifier).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move start_logman into build_setup, use build_teardown in both jobs

Address review comments:
- Move start_logman.yml into build_setup_steps.yml so both jobs get
  it automatically from the shared template.
- Use build_teardown_steps.yml in the main job too (previously only
  the AV job used it), eliminating inline stop_logman +
  PublishPipelineArtifact + clean_ado_folders duplication.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor: make AppVerifier a matrix dimension in build_all_flavors

Replace with_app_verifier boolean with APP_VERIFIER_VALUES list
(default: ["OFF"]). AppVerifier is now a matrix dimension alongside
GBALLOC_LL_TYPE, FSANITIZE_TYPE, BUILD_CONFIGURATION, and ARCH_TYPE.

This gives:
- Central exclusion logic (appverifier+fsanitize, appverifier+non-ctest)
- Automatic BUILD_SUFFIX uniqueness for artifacts/test results
- Single-job template in build_and_run_tests.yml (no two-job emission)

Also:
- Simplify discover_native_tools in build_setup_steps.yml (remove
  redundant ARM64 if/else per review feedback)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix job display name: show (AppVerifier) only for AV jobs

Non-AV jobs now display identically to before (no APP_VERIFIER=OFF
noise). AV jobs show '(AppVerifier)' after 'Build and run tests'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "Fix job display name: show (AppVerifier) only for AV jobs"

This reverts commit 2aea2b9d710b5004969fea01ae6f529587a1fe9a.

* Exclude ARM64 AppVerifier jobs when tests are skipped

No point building the entire project for ARM64 AppVerifier just to
skip all tests. Add exclusion: APP_VERIFIER=ON + ARM64 when
run_arm64_tests is false.

This reduces the job count from 12 to 10 build jobs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Use TCP loopback on Windows instead of AF_UNIX sockets (#14)

* Initial plan

* fix: use TCP loopback on Windows instead of AF_UNIX sockets

On Windows, Node.js (Copilot CLI) uses Named Pipes for IPC, not native
AF_UNIX sockets. Java's ServerSocketChannel with StandardProtocolFamily.UNIX
creates AF_UNIX sockets which are incompatible with Node.js on Windows,
resulting in 'connect EACCES' errors.

This change makes the MCP server use TCP on the loopback interface (127.0.0.1)
on Windows, while keeping Unix domain sockets on Linux/macOS. The lock file
advertises the transport via scheme: 'http' (TCP) or 'pipe' (Unix socket).

Changes:
- McpServer: add TCP constructor and getLocalPort() method
- McpLifecycleManager: detect Windows and select appropriate transport
- LockFileManager: support 'http' scheme for TCP transport
- IdeCliTest: support both TCP and Unix socket connections

feat(benefit-engine): Sprint 1 remediation — catalog, API, frontend & QA fixes

## Catalog Fixes
- Add variableAmounts JSONB field to MasterBenefit for variable monthly
  amounts (e.g., Amex Uber December $35 bonus)
- Fix Amex Gold Resy cadence from ANNUAL to SEMI_ANNUAL
- Add Prisma migration: 20260410000000_sprint1_variable_amounts

## API Fixes
- Add periodStatus filtering to 4 endpoints: dashboard/benefits,
  cards/[id], benefits/recommendations, mobile/sync
- Add hydratePeriodFields utility for manual-add and import flows
  to compute correct period boundaries without engine running
- Add server-side edit/delete guards on benefits/[id] route to
  prevent mutation of engine-managed benefits

## Frontend Fixes
- SummaryStats: deduplicate by composite key (masterBenefitId +
  cardId) to fix double-counting in spouse/multi-card scenarios
- Dashboard benefit card: deduplicate benefit list before rendering
- Card detail page: use unique count for benefit totals
- EditBenefitModal: render read-only mode for engine-managed benefits

## QA Fixes
- Fix Prisma relation name mismatch between schema and queries
- Fix dedup composite key to handle spouse scenario correctly
- Add DELETE guard to prevent deletion of engine-managed benefits

## Infrastructure
- Add BENEFIT_ENGINE_ENABLED=false to railway.json (safe default)
- Document feature flag in .env.example
- Add backfill and cadence-fix scripts for production data migration
- Add benefit-engine specs and QA reports

## Safety
- Feature flag BENEFIT_ENGINE_ENABLED remains OFF (false)
- Zero-risk deployment: all existing behavior unchanged until flag enabled
- Engine-managed benefit guards prevent accidental data corruption
